mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in RSAFS signals strengthening household demand and near-term revenue momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the sales trend is rising.
evidence_refs: fact:latest RSAFS snapshot value was 363806.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if rising 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS crossing above 4.5% raises equity discount rates and tightens credit conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after the threshold is crossed.
evidence_refs: fact:latest FEDFUNDS snapshot value was 4.99 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS observations have mean 20-day S&P 500 returns greater than the unconditional mean or positive returns in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is below 5.0% while FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, tight labor markets and restrictive policy compress profit multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest UNRATE snapshot value was 4.6 and latest FEDFUNDS snapshot value was 4.99
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not produce lower 60-day returns than either single condition alone, or if triggered 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in PAYEMS indicates broad employment income gains that support consumption and earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest PAYEMS snapshot value was 135230.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month PAYEMS growth is followed by negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if the hit rate for positive 5-day returns is below 50% in triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A HOUST decline of more than 10% over 3 months signals weakening housing demand and lower cyclically sensitive earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest HOUST snapshot value was 1957.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST declines below the trigger are followed by 60-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional average in more than 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO combined with CPIAUCSL inflation deceleration signals noninflationary output growth, improving real earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change > 0 and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change falls below its 12-month change
evidence_refs: fact:latest INDPRO snapshot value was 112.0991 and latest CPIAUCSL snapshot value was 201.9
failure_condition: Reject if the joint rising-INDPRO and decelerating-CPI condition has lower average 20-day S&P 500 returns than rising INDPRO alone over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL acceleration above its prior-year pace signals rising inflation pressure that lowers real margins and increases expected policy restraint, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change crosses above its 12-month change by more than 1pp
evidence_refs: fact:latest CPIAUCSL snapshot value was 201.9 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if CPI acceleration triggers are followed by positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns or if triggered returns exceed non-triggered returns by more than 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth over two consecutive quarters signals durable aggregate demand and earnings support, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest GDPC1 snapshot value was 11403.6 as of t-6mo
failure_condition: Reject if two-quarter positive GDPC1 growth produces negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if its 60-day return hit rate is below the unconditional hit rate by more than 5 percentage points.
